archetype: Blastophone Acoustic Yield & Seismic Event Correlation Engine for Deep-Crust Mining
description: A real-time distributed system that ingests high-fidelity acoustic data from piezoelectric blasthole sensors to verify explosive detonation sequencing and simultaneously correlates micro-seismic events with open-pit stability models. Used by geotechnical engineers and blast safety officers to prevent catastrophic slope failures or misfired charges, it is technically hard due to the sub-millisecond synchronization requirements across thousands of sensors in high-noise, high-vibration environments.
rules: MUST adhere to ISO 14644-1 cleanroom standards for sensor data integrity during explosive handling phases to prevent false positives from environmental dust interference.
MUST implement deterministic latency scheduling to ensure acoustic wave arrival times are correlated within 5 microseconds for accurate triangulation of underground voids.
MUST utilize the IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol (PTP) over deterministic Ethernet (TSN) to synchronize geographically dispersed sensor arrays across 5km of operational tunnel.
MUST format all seismic output data according to the QuakeML standard for immediate ingestion by national geological survey databases.
MUST enforce strict role-based access control (RBAC) compliant with MSHA (Mine Safety and Health Administration) regulations regarding blast design approval workflows.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use asynchronous fire-and-forget message queuing for detonation confirmation signals, as the domain requires guaranteed delivery and immediate abort triggers to prevent uncontrolled detonations.
DO NOT rely on standard cloud-based time synchronization (NTP), as network jitter and packet loss in underground tunnels will introduce fatal errors in seismic triangulation accuracy.
DO NOT aggregate raw acoustic waveforms into low-resolution summaries before edge processing, as subtle pre-cursor frequency shifts indicating rock stress fractures would be lost.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing thousands of distributed sensors across variable network latencies in a subterranean environment to achieve sub-microsecond event alignment.
Filtering extreme background acoustic noise from heavy machinery and ventilation systems to isolate specific fracture propagation signatures.
Processing terabytes of high-frequency waveform data in real-time on edge devices with limited compute resources to predict slope instability before it becomes visible.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety and guaranteed real-time performance without garbage collection pauses critical for safety-critical detonation logic.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio with Async-RT — Enables high-concurrency I/O handling for thousands of simultaneous sensor streams while maintaining deterministic scheduling.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB — Optimized for high-velocity time-series ingestion of seismic waveforms and supports complex hypertable queries for historical pattern matching.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster (with persistence)— Essential for buffering transient sensor bursts and facilitating low-latency session state for blast operators.
recommended_stack.message_broker: NATS JetStream — Delivers exactly-once delivery semantics with ultra-low latency for critical abort signals and alert dissemination.
special_services: Edge-based Fast-Fourier Transform (FFT) Pre-processor for real-time frequency domain analysis
Geostatistical Kriging Engine for interpolating rock stress fields from sparse sensor data

compliance_flags: MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57 (Surface/Underground Metal and Nonmetal Mines)
ISO 13849-1 (Safety of Machinery - Safety-Related Parts of Control Systems)
IMCA (International Marine Contractors Association) Guidelines for Underwater Acoustic Monitoring
